rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEWEL DENT REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED 8/30/17
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues remain unaddressed: 1) Notice is insufficient. Form DE-120 is the mandatory notice of hearing for this type of petition (Probate Code Section 17203). 2) The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period (LRSF 14.34.D). 3) The accounting as presented is incomplete, with undescribed disbursements to unknown payees, and disbursements described as being paid from a "non-trust account" which is not explained. For the court to approve the account, bank statements must be filed, the trustee must provide clarity on the transactions unexplained in the account, and trustee must explain from where the "non-trust account disbursements" were paid. 4) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BESSIE L. FRANKLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/19/26. (No earlier availability. Examiner needs more time to review 54-pp petition, as no courtesy copy was provided. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.]) Other issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The first accounting for an estate may not exceed two years. [LRSF 14.34 A.] (2) Original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) The petition caption does not reflect all relief sought; the reduction in distributions to two of the heirs is not shown. [CRC 7.102.] (4) The attorney fee declaration was not filed concurrently with the petition. (5) Paragraph 18 of the petition is unclear. Has the attorney already been paid? (6) Attorney costs must be itemized, showing the date, payee, description, and the amount of each cost. (7) Assets on hand at the beginning of an accounting in a decedent's estate should be the I&A (date of death) values, not the reappraisal for sale values. (8) Refunds for payments made during estate administration are not receipts includable in the fee base. (9) There were disbursements for an "automobile" but there was no automobile inventoried. (10) There were also multiple cash withdrawals without adequate description. (11) For what services were West Coast Legal Services and Books with Numbers paid? Accounting is part of a personal representative's duties; payment to a third party to prepare the court accounting must be deducted from his statutory compensation. (12) Receipts for any preliminary distribution must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.c.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/7/26. It appears that an amendment to the petition (or a fully amended petition) must be filed. Notice of the amendment (or the fully amended petition) must be given after the amending pleading is E-FILED. The Court is aware that a "Request" and "Response" have been filed, which appear to be objections. They will not be considered unless properly filed as objections. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND MATTHEWS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues remain unaddressed: 1) The underlying petition has not been updated to correct the value of the estate's partial interest in the real property. 2) Please provide authority for the request to waive statutory fees in this estate on the condition that they are collected in the related estate. Are petitioner and counsel for petitioner seeking approval in this estate to collect in the related estate if and when the property is sold? Please clarify the request. 3) The response to examiner's notes alludes to an updated proposed order having been lodged, but no such document has been lodged with the court. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/04/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) If an estate is not ready to close within the statutory time set forth in Probate Code Section 12200, the report of status of administration must set forth the status of the estate, including, but not limited to, allegations regarding notice pursuant to Probate Code Section 9202, disclosure of all creditor claims and their dispositions, requests for special notice, and any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution (LRSF 14.35.J.1). 2) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and delivery courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 07/21/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANE MACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/14/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Paragraph 4 of the petition claims that a Final I&A was filed on 12/18/23. The register of actions does not reflect any I&A. (3) Paragraph 3 of the prayer request distribution of all assets to the [d]ecedent's mother...? (In any event, as there were reportedly no assets, the Court will not include an omnibus order, as prayed, when any after-discovered assets could be separate property, which would go 2/3 to Decedent's children.) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/6/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]) Propoosed orders in Probate cases do not require a cover sheet. The cover sheet is for MOTIONS filed in Civil cases.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JACK M. WONG RESUDIARY TRUST, DATED DECEMBER 15, 1996 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues: 1) No EFS-005-CV form is on file to show Albert Wong's counsel consented to electronic service reportedly effected on 02/06/2026. 2) No declaration required by LRSF 14.59(A) and CRC 2.257 is on file to certify that the original financial account statements covering 11/30/2025 will be retained and made available for inspection. In addition, counsel may be asked to address, and the judicial officer may need to determine, whether the Court's 08/04/2021 ex parte order issued in George Wong's probate estate case (PES-16-299859) is required to be set aside as void, in that the order includes a finding that an undivided 1/3 interest in 517 11th Ave is an "asset" of George Wong passing to Sophie Wong from George Wong's estate (and not from the subject trust). If George Wong was a resulting trustee or constructive trustee whose only duty was to convey to the beneficial owner, did George Wong have any transferrable interest in 517 11th Ave that became an asset of his probate estate? If the 08/04/2021 order was void, should John Wong be allowed to collaterally attack the order by this petition, rather than by direct attack with a motion under CCP section 473(d) to set aside in George Wong's probate estate case (or by independent equitable action to set aside the order as a void judgment)? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
